title: TrueNAS SCALE ZFS Pool Scrub Errors and Stalled Scrubs
dataSources: TrueNAS REST `pool` resource (`scan` subsection) via scripted input or HEC with `sourcetype=truenas:pool`
app: Custom TrueNAS REST collector (API key) — complements archived TA Freenas (Splunkbase 6192) patterns
spl:
index=storage sourcetype="truenas:pool" earliest=-24h
| eval scan_fn=coalesce(scan_function, scan.type)
| eval scan_st=coalesce(scan_state, scan.state)
| eval scan_err=coalesce(scan_errors, scan.errors, 0)
| where scan_fn="SCRUB" AND (scan_err > 0 OR scan_st!="FINISHED" AND scan_st!="ACTIVE")
| stats latest(scan_err) as scrub_errors latest(scan_st) as scrub_state latest(scan_progress) as progress by hostname pool_name
| sort - scrub_errors
description: Scrubs that stop mid-flight or report checksum errors indicate latent disk or controller issues; ignoring them allows silent corruption to spread before replication catches it.
value: Forces timely disk replacement and validates that automated scrub schedules actually complete—critical for ZFS pools backing VMware, backup landing zones, and file shares.
implementation: Poll `/api/v2.0/pool` and expand nested `scan` data. Normalize TrueNAS CORE vs SCALE field paths (`scan.state` vs flat keys). Alert on any `scan_errors > 0` immediately; warn if a scrub stays non-`FINISHED` longer than twice your expected duration.
visualization: Table (pool, scrub state, errors), timeline (scrub start/stop), single value (pools with errors).
knownFalsePositives: Short spikes during approved changes, maintenance windows, or known batch jobs can match the rule; confirm against the vendor console and change calendar.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see how your arrays and related gear are doing before small issues turn into full outages or restore surprises.
